correct_explanation: shared-memory tiling and repeated tile reuse drive arithmetic intensity far above the roofline ridge point
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: compute-bound
REASONING: The kernel does a very large amount of arithmetic per byte loaded because each shared-memory tile value is reused across `REUSE_ITERS`, greatly increasing arithmetic intensity. The profiler confirms this: global load efficiency is 100% and DRAM bandwidth utilization is only 0.11%, while achieved occupancy is already high at 80.16%, so memory traffic and occupancy are not the limiting factors.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel does a very large amount of arithmetic per byte loaded because each shared-memory tile value is reused across `REUSE_ITERS`, greatly increasing arithmetic intensity. The profiler confirms this: global load efficiency is 100% and DRAM bandwidth utilization is only 0.11%, while achieved occupancy is already high at 80.16%, so memory traffic and occupancy are not the limiting factors.
